title: I connected Claude to a real brokerage - created DCA bot,  placing live trades from plain English
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
This broke my brain a little.



Public has an MCP server that connects Claude directly to their trading API. Not "generate code that calls an API." Claude actually calls get\_quotes, preflight\_order, and place\_order on a live funded account.



I typed one prompt along these lines:



"$2,500/month across VOO, QQQ, BTC, ETH, AAPL. Every two weeks. Buy more on dips. Pull back when extended. Boost equity buys when VIX is above 25."



Claude:



1. Pulled real-time quotes (figured out equities and crypto need separate calls on its own)

2. Calculated fractional share amounts per allocation

3. Web searched for current VIX to check the fear signal

4. Preflighted all 5 orders against my account

5. Returned exact costs — $0 commission on stocks, $1.12 per crypto trade, $1,252.24 total



Best part: it tried limit orders first, hit a 400 error (dollar-amount orders must be market type), diagnosed it, switched order types, and succeeded. Zero human intervention.



There's no code anywhere. No script. No server. The entire trading system is a conversation.



MCP is the thing that makes this work -it's a protocol that gives AI actual tool access, not just the ability to write code about tools. This is the most practical use case I've seen for it.



Happy to answer questions about the setup.

https://reddit.com/link/1ryajvs/video/lw2kvmp942qg1/player

comments.author: d4mations
comments.body: How can access the mcp server?

comments.author: j_hes_
comments.body: You’re going to get your account smoked. In plain English.

replies.author: Del_Phoenix
replies.body: I mean, it's not even doing any trade logic. Basically just a text to action converter.

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: All trading systems are text to action converters. The question is how smart the text is. A quant's 10,000-line strategy is text. A Bloomberg rule is text, so is a fix protocol order. The difference is my 'text' can derive reason about regime, vol surface, GEX, options flow, and position sizing simultaneously and rewrite its own logic as conditions change. A Python if-statement can't do that.

replies.author: Del_Phoenix
replies.body: Oh cool, well I agree with the first guy then.

comments.author: Wide-Firefighter6524
comments.body: cool demo but letting an LLM place live trades with zero human confirmation is insane to me. one hallucination and it buys 10x what you wanted. at minimum add a confirmation step before execution. the "it diagnosed a 400 error and fixed it" part sounds great until it "fixes" something by placing a completely wrong order

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: Don’t completely disagree. Why it’s important to have hard guardrails in place- all of my risk engines are battle tested and I run more complex stuff from server side

replies.author: Lonely_Corgi_728
replies.body: Then Claude will just say “Sorry, my mistake!”.

replies.author: Live-Gazelle521
replies.body: Claude - Oh. Sorry I lost all your money. Should I find the nearest Wendy’s for you to take a position?

replies.author: DoubleFar6023
replies.body:
there are many ways around hallucinations, one i use is  having another instance confirm the strategy , while it can still happen , odds of a bad trade are significantly less.

i have trained micro models for trade confirmations , the run and confirm the main trading instance. you can have as many as you have resources.

comments.author: DreamfulTrader
comments.body: What is the real benefit vs not being lazy and opening the broker, looking at the price and buy directly?

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: Trades are executed via API- faster, less slippage, no human error/bias

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: There is a reason they never show the prompt, code, or anything else. It doesn’t work well.

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: That was the whole point of the video…so you can see it

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: I overlooked it, thinking it was an advertisement.  Thanks for pointing it out.  Will be interesting to reproduce.

comments.author: ztbwl
comments.body:
Something‘s missing:
How would buying more on dips work? Your session is long ended when the dip happens. There is no scheduling or recurring evaluation to find the correct moment for your trades without any code on your side.

comments.author: preimumpossy
comments.body: Wow you posted this in so many forums...

comments.author: NateDoggzTN
comments.body:
I’ve spent the last 3 years building a fully autonomous local system (\*\*AutoTrade\*\*) and I’ve tried the Alpaca/MCP route. It’s "brain-breaking" for the first 10 minutes, then the reality of \*\*Market Regime\*\* hits you.  
  
The [Public.com](http://Public.com) MCP server is real, but here is why I call bull on this being a long-term "set and forget" strategy:  
  
1.  \*\*The "Vibe" Trap\*\*: Telling an LLM to "Buy more on dips" is dangerous. LLMs don't have a consistent definition of a "dip" or "extended" unless you give them a 500-line prompt with ATR-based thresholds. Prompting is not engineering.  
2.  \*\*Order Type Diagnosis\*\*: You mentioned it "diagnosed" a 400 error and switched to market orders. That’s cool until it "diagnoses" a slippage error by doubling down or market-selling into a flash crash.   
3.  \*\*No Data Foundation\*\*: Trading is 90% data cleaning. In my project, we use DuckDB/Parquet for local OHLCV bars because relying on an LLM to \`get\_quotes\` via a 3rd party tool in real-time is the "unsexy" way to lose money to latency and API timeouts.  
  
\*\*My take\*\*: This is a great "Hello World" for MCP, but a terrible way to manage $2,500/month. If you want a real DCA bot, write the logic in a script so it's \*\*deterministic\*\*. I have a repo (\*\*natedoggzCD/YfinanceDownloader\*\*) that focuses on getting the clean data first—build your logic on that, not on a conversation.  
  
The system has to justify \*why\* it wants to act, not just \*what\* it wants to do. Vibes don't justify trades.

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body: Thanks for your take- not claiming any “set and forget” or long term validity here. It’s just a simple example of the capabilities of the MCP

comments.author: Additional-Stock7651
comments.body:
Cool Idea  
Hmm how much does it cost? and how do you earn can we have a breakdown?  
Like how much you deposited for start trading  
How much Claude credit spent vs how much we can earn?

How does it compare to trend signal AI like airradar dot pro?  
Having AI trade 24/7 especially on crypto sounds like a dream com through.
